case.title: The House Never Mourns
case.subtitle: A noir casino-hotel murder where surveillance tells the wrong truth.
case.setting: L'Auberge Hotel and Casino, Baton Rouge, Louisiana

finalQuestions.label: Why did the killer do it?
options: To stop Adrian from exposing comp fraud, route manipulation, and the larger pattern

finalQuestions.label: How was the murder committed?
options: Adrian fell accidentally while intoxicated in a restricted area
Adrian was drugged through his drink, redirected through restricted access, and staged into a fall
Adrian was sober and simply pushed over the edge
Adrian was injured elsewhere and moved to the mezzanine after death

finalQuestions.label: What best proves the fall was staged?
options: The proof Adrian was impaired before the fall

hints.1: The building's story fails first where the rail and body mechanics stop supporting a clean accident.
hints.2: The last drink matters because it explains why the fall story feels medically incomplete.
hints.3: The surveillance package is dangerous because it is truthful about the wrong part of the route.
hints.4: The real question is not whether Adrian fell. It is whether he still owned his movement before he fell.
hints.5: The right motive explains why this case feels more organized than the first two.

suspects.name: Celeste Marrow
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Worked closely with Adrian on VIP lounge operations and guest movement
suspects.status_late: Procedural authority starts looking like murder cover

suspects.name: Reggie Sloan
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Adrian accused him of being careless about surveillance blind spots
suspects.status_late: Corrupt, but not the architect of this death

suspects.name: Marisol Vane
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Adrian threatened to report her for off-book guest perks and access favors
suspects.status_late: Suspicious social cover, wrong movement pattern

suspects.name: Owen Hart
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Adrian escalated maintenance complaints tied to service corridors and restricted doors
suspects.status_early: Infrastructure access makes him look perfect

suspects.name: Nadia Price
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Adrian questioned her about premium drink orders and room-charge anomalies
suspects.status_early: Drink access makes her look dangerous fast
suspects.status_late: Could touch the drink, not the whole route

suspects.name: Victor Shaw
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Adrian criticized security handling of VIP incidents
suspects.status_late: Protecting the building, not killing for it

evidence_cufflink_rail.title: Broken Cufflink and Rail Mark
evidence_cufflink_rail.desc: The mezzanine service-edge rail shows a scrape and a broken cufflink inconsistent with a simple drunken overbalance.
evidence_cufflink_rail.short: The edge evidence argues with the easy fall story.

evidence_martini_log.title: Espresso Martini Service Record
evidence_martini_log.desc: Premium lounge service records show Adrian received an espresso martini shortly before leaving the lounge.
evidence_martini_log.short: The last drink becomes the likely delivery window.

evidence_glass_residue.title: Glass Residue Swab
evidence_glass_residue.desc: Residue from Adrian's glass suggests chemical adulteration rather than ordinary alcohol alone.
evidence_glass_residue.short: Adrian's last drink was likely compromised.

evidence_corridor_access_ping.title: Restricted Corridor Access Ping
evidence_corridor_access_ping.desc: A restricted staff-side access event occurred shortly before Adrian's fall.
evidence_corridor_access_ping.short: Someone put Adrian where guests were not supposed to be.

evidence_conflict_statement.title: Quiet Conflict Statement
evidence_conflict_statement.desc: Simone heard Adrian tell Celeste, "I'm not burying this for you."
evidence_conflict_statement.short: Adrian and Celeste were in serious conflict before the death.

evidence_housekeeping_sighting.title: Housekeeping Corridor Sighting
evidence_housekeeping_sighting.desc: Tori saw a sharply dressed staff member supporting a man who looked chemically off, not merely drunk.
evidence_housekeeping_sighting.short: Adrian was guided, not freely wandering.

evidence_medical_impairment_flag.title: Medical Impairment Flag
evidence_medical_impairment_flag.desc: Adrian was chemically impaired before the fall, making the intoxication-accident story inadequate.
evidence_medical_impairment_flag.short: The fall happened after Adrian was already compromised.

evidence_audit_packet.title: Audit Request Packet
evidence_audit_packet.desc: Adrian requested premium comp records, restricted-route logs, and incident overlap reports shortly before his death.
evidence_audit_packet.short: Adrian was about to expose more than ordinary fraud.

evidence_symbol_comparison.title: Symbol-Marked Incident Comparison
evidence_symbol_comparison.desc: Adrian's private file includes the same black symbol connected to the prior nightlife deaths.
evidence_symbol_comparison.short: The pattern is no longer theory. It is evidence.

evidence_surveillance_gap.title: Surveillance Angle Gap
evidence_surveillance_gap.desc: Visible footage captures Adrian leaving the lounge area, but not the restricted turn that leads toward the service mezzanine.
evidence_surveillance_gap.short: The camera story is true, but incomplete.

evidence_route_timing.title: Elevator vs Service Stair Timing
evidence_route_timing.desc: Celeste's stated route fits poorly compared with a quicker service-stair route.
evidence_route_timing.short: Celeste's procedural story may be physically wrong.

evidence_tray_assignment.title: Beverage Tray Assignment Record
evidence_tray_assignment.desc: Nadia had service responsibility for the drink zone, but not exclusive custody of Adrian's last drink.
evidence_tray_assignment.short: Nadia could touch the drink, but not alone.

evidence_maintenance_timestamp.title: Maintenance Ticket Timestamp
evidence_maintenance_timestamp.desc: Owen's maintenance ping places him in infrastructure response during part of the critical window.
evidence_maintenance_timestamp.short: Owen looks better once the timestamps matter.

evidence_guest_escort_log.title: Guest Escort Log
evidence_guest_escort_log.desc: Marisol's VIP escort activity is partially supported by guest movement records.
evidence_guest_escort_log.short: Marisol's polished movement has some hard support.

evidence_security_delay.title: Security Response Delay
evidence_security_delay.desc: Victor's response timeline is dirty and self-protective, but not well aligned with the actual death route.
evidence_security_delay.short: Victor protects optics better than truth.

evidence_pocket_recorder.title: Adrian's Pocket Recorder Clip
evidence_pocket_recorder.desc: A short clip records Adrian saying, "same accident language, different venue."
evidence_pocket_recorder.short: Adrian knew the pattern before he died.

evidence_fabric_transfer.title: Smudged Fabric Transfer
evidence_fabric_transfer.desc: A fabric transfer on the mezzanine edge suggests close physical contact immediately before the fall.
evidence_fabric_transfer.short: Someone was close enough to help the fall happen.

evidence_backstation_still.title: Lounge Backstation Camera Still
evidence_backstation_still.desc: A still image places Celeste near Adrian's drink path at the wrong moment for her stated duties.
evidence_backstation_still.short: Celeste was near the drink when she says she was elsewhere.

evidence_comp_sheet.title: Internal Comp Exception Sheet
evidence_comp_sheet.desc: Celeste's name appears repeatedly in the exact comp exception cluster Adrian was reviewing.
evidence_comp_sheet.short: Adrian's records and Celeste's records overlap too neatly.

evidence_reggie_side_hustle.title: Blind Spot Side Hustle
evidence_reggie_side_hustle.desc: Reggie sold camera timing habits and blind-spot info to people who wanted to exploit the building.

evidence_owen_signoff.title: Falsified Inspection Sign-Off
evidence_owen_signoff.desc: Owen falsified a minor inspection sign-off connected to the service mezzanine.
evidence_owen_signoff.short: He lies on infrastructure. That does not make him the killer.

evidence_marisol_perks.title: Off-Book Guest Perks
evidence_marisol_perks.desc: Marisol had been running unauthorized guest favors and VIP access perks.
evidence_marisol_perks.short: Elegant and shady. Still not homicide.

evidence_victor_reports.title: Buried Incident Reports
evidence_victor_reports.desc: Victor suppressed minor incidents to protect casino optics.

evidence_nadia_comps.title: Unlogged Drink Comps
evidence_nadia_comps.desc: Nadia covered for premium drink comps that were not logged correctly.
evidence_nadia_comps.short: Drink wrongdoing makes Nadia look worse than she is.

public: Adrian is seen in the premium lounge.
Adrian is found below the mezzanine service edge.

notes: Visible surveillance covers the guest path well but not the restricted turn.
Staff-side movement can look routine even when it is fatal.
The mezzanine edge reads as accidental until you compare body mechanics and route control.

floors.label: Casino Floor
floors.subtitle: Public glamour and the last fully visible route.

routes.label: Visible departure path
notes: What cameras capture before the real route diverges.

zones.label: Premium Lounge
notes: Last confirmed drink location.
Adrian and Celeste conflict here.

zones.label: Casino Floor
notes: Luxury noise helps the building feel innocent.

zones.label: VIP Transition
notes: Guest and staff-facing movement blur here.

zones.label: Elevator Bank
notes: Believable route. Slower than the true one.

zones.label: Guest Corridor
notes: Last public-facing movement before Adrian vanishes.

floors.label: Hotel Connector
floors.subtitle: The place where the camera story starts lying by omission.

routes.label: Restricted redirect route
notes: The true path is faster and quieter than guest movement.

zones.label: Hotel Corridor Junction
notes: Where guest logic and staff logic begin to split.

zones.label: Service Turn
notes: Blind transition point not cleanly covered by visible surveillance.

zones.label: Housekeeping Pass
notes: Witness Tori's sighting area.

zones.label: Staff Stairs
notes: Fast vertical route that fits the true movement better than elevators.

zones.label: Private Access Door
notes: Staff-only access normal guests should never use.

routes.label: Final staging route
notes: The building's preferred lie is finished here.

zones.label: Service Landing
notes: Adrian arrives here no longer moving like a healthy man.

zones.label: Maintenance Edge
notes: Infrastructure red herring pointing toward Owen.

zones.label: Restricted Walk
notes: Close-contact route just before the fall setup.

zones.label: Camera Gap Angle
notes: Surveillance expectation is weaponized here.

zones.label: Fall Site
notes: The building's preferred lie: intoxication, misstep, done.

scene_301_opening.title: L'Auberge After Midnight
scene_301_opening.text: Luxury buildings always sound best when they are hiding something. L'Auberge keeps glowing, and Adrian Bell is already being converted into an administrative tragedy. The first story is efficient: too much drink, wrong access point, one bad step. But efficiency is often how institutions bury intention.

scene_302_body_reveal.title: Below the Service Edge
scene_302_body_reveal.text: Adrian lies below the service mezzanine in a place meant for movement, not memory. The building wants a clean overbalance story. The body and edge do not cooperate.

scene_303_rail_mark.title: Broken Cufflink and Rail
scene_303_rail_mark.text: The service-edge rail carries a fresh scrape and a broken cufflink fragment. This was not a graceful overbalance. Something met the edge wrong before gravity finished the job.

scene_304_initial_story.title: The House Story
scene_304_initial_story.text: Security and management are already converging around the same phrase: intoxicated restricted-access fall. It sounds complete before anyone asks who chose the access, who saw the intoxication, or why the most useful camera angle stops where it does.

scene_305_suspect_board_intro.title: Who Owned the Night?
scene_305_suspect_board_intro.text: A lounge manager with procedural authority. A surveillance tech who knows what cameras miss. A hostess with polished access. A facilities engineer with infrastructure cover. A beverage server who could touch the last drink. A security supervisor who knows how buildings explain themselves. This is a system full of suspects.

scene_307_round1_checkpoint.title: The Easy Story Fails
scene_307_round1_checkpoint.prompt: What first weakens the intoxicated-fall explanation?
answers: The venue is upscale enough that this would be embarrassing
The rail and cufflink evidence do not fit a simple overbalance
Security wants the case closed quickly
Adrian worked with too many staff members
scene_307_round1_checkpoint.correctText: The building's story depends on a clean accidental fall. The rail and cufflink evidence make that story physically less believable.
scene_307_round1_checkpoint.wrongText: That may make the story feel suspicious, but the first real crack comes from the physical scene itself.

scene_308_nadia_intro.title: Nadia Price
scene_308_nadia_intro.text: Nadia looks like every late-shift worker who has already been blamed in her head three times before the question even lands.

scene_309_simone_intro.title: Simone Baptiste
scene_309_simone_intro.text: Simone heard enough to know the room changed after Adrian's conversation with Celeste.

scene_310_celeste_intro.title: Celeste Marrow
scene_310_celeste_intro.text: Celeste wears authority like the building loaned it to her directly.

scene_311_marisol_intro.title: Marisol Vane
scene_311_marisol_intro.text: Marisol is too polished to look harmless and too socially trained to look rattled.

scene_312_service_records.title: Lounge Service Records
scene_312_service_records.text: Drink logs, tray assignments, and exception entries leave behind the small mechanical truths luxury venues rely on. Adrian's last drink stands out because normal service can become the cleanest weapon.

scene_314_round2_checkpoint.title: The Last Drink Matters
scene_314_round2_checkpoint.prompt: Why does Adrian's last drink matter more than the fall alone?
answers: Because expensive lounge drinks are carefully tracked
Because it explains impairment before the fall
Because Nadia served it personally
Because Adrian always drank espresso martinis
scene_314_round2_checkpoint.correctText: The fall only matters fully once you know Adrian's body was already being changed before it reached the edge.
scene_314_round2_checkpoint.wrongText: That detail may matter, but the key is that the last drink can explain why the fall story fails medically and physically.

scene_315_reggie_intro.title: Reggie Sloan
scene_315_reggie_intro.text: Reggie stopped believing in clean truth around the same time he learned how many monitors a building can hide behind.

scene_316_ellis_intro.title: Ellis Crowe
scene_316_ellis_intro.text: Ellis remembers movement well. He trusts his conclusions less.

scene_317_route_review.title: What the Cameras Miss
scene_317_route_review.text: The surveillance package is not fake. That is what makes it dangerous. It captures enough to satisfy a building that wants simplicity, but not the restricted turn where guest movement stops and controlled staff movement begins.

scene_319_round3_checkpoint.title: The Camera Lie
scene_319_round3_checkpoint.prompt: What is the biggest mistake in trusting the camera story?
answers: It was recorded by Reggie Sloan
It captures the visible route, not the real one
It is too grainy to be useful
It only shows the lounge
scene_319_round3_checkpoint.correctText: The footage helps the building because it shows enough to appear complete while hiding the turn that matters most.
scene_319_round3_checkpoint.wrongText: The deeper problem is structural: the footage supports an incomplete route as if it were the whole truth.

scene_320_tori_intro.title: Tori Nguyen
scene_320_tori_intro.text: Tori is disturbed by the fact that helping and hurting can wear the same posture.

scene_321_royce_intro.title: Dr. Helena Royce
scene_321_royce_intro.text: Dr. Royce does not dramatize. That makes her useful in a building addicted to elegant explanations.

scene_322_mezzanine_trace.title: Close Contact at the Edge
scene_322_mezzanine_trace.text: The mezzanine edge gives up two betrayals: a fabric transfer where Adrian should have been alone, and a placement pattern that reads less like a free man's bad decision and more like an impaired man's guided end.

scene_323_round4_checkpoint.title: Guided, Not Wandering
scene_323_round4_checkpoint.prompt: What best explains Adrian's final movement?
answers: He was drunk and exploring a restricted area alone
He was impaired and guided through staff-only access
He chased a staff member up the mezzanine
He mistook the mezzanine for a guest exit
scene_323_round4_checkpoint.correctText: The witness posture, medical sequence, and edge evidence all align: Adrian was not wandering freely when he died.
scene_323_round4_checkpoint.wrongText: That theory keeps the building comfortable, but it fails the witness, the chemistry, and the contact evidence.

scene_324_ben_intro.title: Ben Gallow
scene_324_ben_intro.text: Ben came expecting financial damage and found homicide instead.

scene_325_records_reveal.title: What Adrian Was About to Expose
scene_325_records_reveal.text: Adrian died because too many secrets started overlapping under one name: comp exceptions, restricted-route access, repeated incident language, and the same black symbol appearing where it never should.

scene_326_celeste_followup.title: Celeste Under Pressure
scene_326_celeste_followup.text: Celeste does not crack. She refines. She insists Adrian mistook institutional repetition for conspiracy and was simply running out of time to embarrass himself properly.

scene_326b_case_synthesis.title: Procedure as Cover
scene_326b_case_synthesis.text: Adrian was in conflict with Celeste. His last drink was compromised. The visible camera route stops before the real danger begins. A staff-only access point moved him where guests should never be. Witnesses saw him guided, not wandering. His records threatened not only fraud, but a pattern tied to earlier deaths.

scene_327_round5_checkpoint.title: Why Celeste Couldn't Let Him Live
scene_327_round5_checkpoint.prompt: Why was Adrian killed that night?
answers: He was going to embarrass Nadia over drink logs
He had enough to expose comp fraud, route manipulation, and the larger nightlife death pattern
He was about to fire Marisol for VIP perks
He discovered Owen's false inspection reports
scene_327_round5_checkpoint.correctText: Adrian had become dangerous because he was no longer seeing isolated irregularities. He was seeing the system.
scene_327_round5_checkpoint.wrongText: The real motive must explain why Adrian's records threatened more than one person and more than one venue.

scene_328_final_accusation.title: Name the Killer
scene_328_final_accusation.text: The casino keeps moving. Luxury has already started sanding the event into something operational. Now you decide whether Adrian Bell becomes one more elegant accident — or the case that breaks the pattern into the light.

ending_perfect.title: Perfect Solve
ending_perfect.text: You refused the house story. Celeste Marrow used the architecture of luxury against Adrian Bell: drink service, restricted routing, surveillance expectation, and a staged fall packaged as an administrative tragedy. This one is proof of design.

ending_solved.title: Case Solved
ending_solved.text: Celeste Marrow used the building's procedures as camouflage for murder. The route, chemistry, and records finally lock together, and the pattern is still operating.

ending_partial.title: Close, But Not Clean
ending_partial.text: You found the structure, but not tightly enough. The drink, the route, the surveillance gap, and the records were all there, but luxury buildings are good at blurring certainty into acceptable doubt.

ending_failed.title: The House Keeps Its Story
ending_failed.text: You let the building win. Adrian Bell did not die because the venue was careless. He died because someone inside it understood exactly how much truth a luxury institution needs to fake an accident.

topics.label: Adrian's Movement
topics.response: He was moving with purpose. Quick, yes. Sloppy, no.

topics.label: Where Was He Going?
topics.response: Toward the corridor break where guest movement starts losing dignity and staff movement starts getting fast.

topics.label: His Demeanor
topics.response: Annoyed. Focused. Like a man already in the middle of a problem.

interview_tori.name: Tori Nguyen
interview_tori.intro: I thought somebody was helping him.

topics.label: What You Saw
topics.response: A sharply dressed staff person supporting a man who looked wrong in the body.

topics.label: Staff Appearance
topics.response: Too polished for housekeeping or facilities. Lounge-management polished.

topics.label: Help or Harm?
topics.response: If someone is already weakened, help and harm can look identical from across a corridor.

interview_simone.name: Simone Baptiste
interview_simone.intro: I heard enough to know the room changed.

topics.label: The Conflict
topics.response: Adrian told Celeste, "I'm not burying this for you."

topics.label: Tone
topics.response: Quiet, which is worse. Quiet conflict carries itself somewhere private.

topics.label: Who Was Nearby?
topics.response: Nadia was on service. Celeste was circulating. Marisol was doing guest-facing perfection.

interview_royce.name: Dr. Helena Royce
interview_royce.intro: The body is less interested in luxury than the building is.

topics.label: Chemical Impairment
topics.response: Adrian was impaired before the fall. Alcohol alone does not explain the body.

topics.label: Fall Sequence
topics.response: The fall is real. The problem is that his body had already started failing him before gravity got involved.

topics.label: Drink Delivery
topics.response: The compromised drink is the most practical explanation for impairment beginning before the edge.

interview_ben.name: Ben Gallow
interview_ben.intro: Adrian had moved beyond suspicion. He was into documentation.

topics.label: What Adrian Requested
topics.response: Comp exception records, route logs, and overlapping incident summaries. He was building a case.

topics.label: The Symbol
topics.response: If you see that mark twice, it stops being coincidence and starts being architecture.

topics.label: Comp Exceptions
topics.response: Celeste's name keeps surfacing in the same exception cluster Adrian marked.

interview_celeste.name: Celeste Marrow
interview_celeste.intro: If you intend to confuse institutional complexity with guilt, this conversation will be long.

topics.label: Your Route
topics.response: Lounge closeout, guest smoothing, staff release, then incident coordination.

topics.label: Adrian Bell
topics.response: Capable. Increasingly convinced every irregularity was a coded message to him personally.

topics.label: Comp Records
topics.response: Premium hospitality runs on exceptions.

topics.label: Press Further: Service Stair Timing
topics.response: Buildings this large reward flexibility. If logs don't match my exact stride, that says more about logs than about me.

topics.label: Press Further: Adrian's Pattern Theory
topics.response: Adrian mistook repetition for revelation.

interview_reggie.name: Reggie Sloan
interview_reggie.intro: Everybody trusts cameras until they learn cameras mostly document the parts buildings admit exist.

topics.label: Surveillance Coverage
topics.response: Coverage is broad. Complete is a fantasy management sells to itself.

topics.label: Adrian Bell
topics.response: He thought every gap was a moral failure.

topics.label: Press Further: Blind Spot Side Hustle
topics.response: I sold habits, not bodies.

interview_marisol.name: Marisol Vane
interview_marisol.intro: Luxury venues depend on selective generosity.

topics.label: Your VIP Route
topics.response: Guests, elevators, floor smoothing, private reassurance.

topics.label: Adrian Bell
topics.response: Adrian preferred systems to people.

topics.label: Press Further: Off-Book Perks
topics.response: I protected important guests from unnecessary friction.

topics.label: Guest Escort Log
topics.response: My movement is still tethered to real guests and real timestamps.

interview_owen.name: Owen Hart
interview_owen.intro: Infrastructure always looks guilty after someone dies in a restricted space.

topics.label: Service Mezzanine
topics.response: That does not mean every bad thing there belongs to me.

topics.label: Conflict with Adrian
topics.response: He escalated everything. It made him exhausting.

topics.label: Maintenance Call
topics.response: I had a real door fault to check.

topics.label: Press Further: Falsified Sign-Off
topics.response: Bad judgment. Wrong homicide.

interview_nadia.name: Nadia Price
interview_nadia.intro: I'm the easiest person to blame from the drink angle.

topics.label: Adrian's Drink
topics.response: His last drink came through my zone. That does not mean I had it alone every second.

topics.label: Adrian Bell
topics.response: He was starting to ask uncomfortable questions.

topics.label: Press Further: Unlogged Drink Comps
topics.response: Late-shift survival is not how this murder works.

topics.label: Tray Assignment Record
topics.response: My station touched the drink. It did not own the whole chain.

interview_victor.name: Victor Shaw
interview_victor.intro: Security exists to keep the building functional, not emotionally satisfying.

topics.label: Your Incident Response
topics.response: Guest altercation, floor response, optics management.

topics.label: Adrian Bell
topics.response: He wanted clean institutions. I wanted functional ones.

topics.label: Press Further: Buried Reports
topics.response: I buried small messes to protect the house.
